title: CI: Use local cache registry
body:
As of https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/357 our CI VMs include a local registry preloaded with all(*) images used in tests.

(*) where "all" means "most".

This PR sets up CI such that it will use that registry.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

@containers/podman-maintainers this is getting close. There's one question I can't decide and would like help with: how to handle local developer-workstation runs:
1. same as CI: force devs to set up and run a local registry. This is what my third commit is intended for. Or:
2. same as how things are today: local-dev runs will use actual real-world registries.

Both options stink. Option 1 imposes work and complexity on anyone running tests. Option 2 would require maintenance of almost-duplicate `registries.conf` files, and a mechanism for tests to decide which to use when, and conditionals for a handful of tests that behave differently (403 vs 404 on no-such-image pulls); and of course local-environment runs will not be testing the same as CI.

As it stands right now, this PR implements option 1. Your feedback would be most welcome.

> 2. same as how things are today: local-dev runs will use actual real-world registries.

I prefer 2 but can be convinced that 1 is better. However if a local registry is required then IMO it must be part of the test setup and not user responsibility. If I run `test/apiv2/test-apiv2|make localintegration|hack/bats` today that must keep working and I should not have to run another script manually before that. Given that I do not see how this would deal with pulling images for the local registry? I do not want to wait a very long time pulling images the first time when I run a system test which only ever needs one tiny image.

>if a local registry is required then IMO it must be part of the test setup and not user responsibility

Fair point, and that's close to impossible. And if it _were_ possible it would be unbearable to wait 20-60m for the cache to preload. Okay, Option 1 it is then.

This implements option 1: tests in CI will use the cache registry, but anyone running tests on a laptop will still get the old behavior (pulling from real registries). The switch is `$CI_USE_REGISTRY_CACHE`
If this is acceptable, I think it just needs documentation, and one more CI VM build

Good idea. 

/hold

Let's not merge this yet. I saw [this bud flake](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4673065106276352/html/bud-podman-fedora-40-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--00319) over the weekend and I want to understand it better.

/lgtm
I will let @edsantiago cancel the hold
